skill.name: content-evaluation-framework
skill.description: Evaluates educational chapters, lessons, and learning materials with a weighted rubric for accuracy, pedagogy, writing quality, structure, AI-first teaching, and constitution compliance.
skill.summary: Apply a structured rubric to review educational content quality and readiness.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: The 73 static findings were reviewed in context. The external command, weak cryptography, sensitive file, and reconnaissance matches are Markdown formulas, inline paths, rubric language, and checklist text rather than executable behavior. No evidence found of prompt injection, data exfiltration, malicious command execution, or network callbacks; the only confirmed risk factor is expected local file reading for content evaluation.

low_findings.title: Expected Local File Review Scope
low_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to read user-selected lesson files, local specs, constitution files, and included rubric references. This is necessary for the evaluator workflow, but users should only run it in workspaces where content review is intended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited instructions explicitly require reading local content and context files. This is an intended capability, not malicious behavior, so the concern is limited to normal workspace privacy scope.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick matches occur in Markdown code fences, formulas, and inline file references. No executable script, shell invocation, or user-controlled command path was found in the cited context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are visible Markdown instructions or report templates. They do not define executable code and do not instruct shell execution.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto, Sensitive File, and Reconnaissance Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: The high and low severity static matches refer to rubric headings, score criteria, checklist rules, and ordinary file path examples. No weak cryptographic API use, secret access, system enumeration, or network probing behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited context is plain rubric and checklist prose. The only .env mention is an example of project organization, and the security terms instruct reviewers to reject secrets rather than access them.

content.value_statement: Educational drafts are hard to judge consistently across technical accuracy, teaching quality, and structure. This skill applies a weighted rubric and compliance checklist to produce clear review findings and revision priorities.
actual_capabilities: Scores educational chapters and lessons across five weighted quality categories.
Checks constitution compliance as a pass or fail gate before weighted scoring.
Produces evidence-based findings with strengths, weaknesses, and recommendations.
Uses included rubric, checklist, and report template references for consistency.
Supports quick spot checks, mid-draft reviews, and full publication readiness reviews.
limitations: Requires access to the content and relevant local project context for best results.
Does not execute or verify code examples unless the user separately asks for testing.
Uses the included constitution model, which may not match every organization.
Cannot replace subject matter expert review for specialized technical claims.

use_cases.title: Review a Lesson Draft
use_cases.description: Evaluate a draft lesson for accuracy, learner fit, structure, and actionable improvements before final editing.

use_cases.title: Gate Publication Readiness
use_cases.description: Apply the constitution checklist and weighted score to decide whether a chapter can proceed to publication.

use_cases.title: Improve AI Learning Activities
use_cases.description: Assess whether AI exercises support learner understanding instead of answer generation.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Lesson Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one short lesson section.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this lesson section with the content evaluation framework. Focus on the clearest strengths, the most important gaps, and three practical revisions.

prompt_templates.title: Full Chapter Evaluation
prompt_templates.scenario: Standard review before a chapter moves to validation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this completed chapter against the full rubric. Include constitution compliance, category scores, evidence, and prioritized next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Draft Versions
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the revised draft with the earlier review findings. Identify resolved issues, remaining blockers, and any new quality concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Publication Gate Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced quality gate for curriculum release.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a publication readiness audit for this chapter. Use the constitution gate first, then score each category and give a pass, conditional pass, or fail verdict.

output_examples.input: A writer asks for feedback on a nearly finished lesson about Python functions.
output: Overall verdict: Conditional pass with a Good score.
Main strengths: clear examples, useful exercises, and strong learning objectives.
Priority revisions: add type hints to every function and strengthen the AI reflection activity.

output_examples.input: A curriculum lead asks whether a chapter is ready for publication.
output: Constitution compliance: Fail because one critical requirement is missing.
Weighted scoring should pause until the blocking issue is fixed.
Required action: add tested examples with expected output and update the review evidence.

output_examples.input: An instructional designer asks for an AI-first teaching review.
output: The lesson uses AI prompts, but most ask for direct answers instead of learner reasoning.
Recommended revision: redesign prompts to ask for explanations, comparisons, and error analysis.

best_practices: Provide the target content, learning objectives, and relevant specs in the same request.
Ask for evidence with line references so each score can be verified.
Run a quick review before a full gate review to catch structural issues early.
anti_patterns: Do not use the final score without reading the supporting evidence.
Do not skip constitution compliance when reviewing publication readiness.
Do not ask for broad quality feedback without sharing the draft or evaluation context.

faq.question: What content can this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: It is designed for educational chapters, lessons, and curriculum materials, especially technical learning content.

faq.question: Does it decide whether content can be published?
faq.answer: It can recommend pass, conditional pass, or fail based on the included rubric and constitution checklist.

faq.question: Can it review partial drafts?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports spot checks and mid-draft reviews, but final scoring works best on complete content.

faq.question: Does it test code examples?
faq.answer: No. It reviews code quality and evidence unless the user separately requests execution or verification.

faq.question: Why does it need local files?
faq.answer: It compares the draft against specs, learning objectives, templates, and constitution rules when those files are available.

faq.question: Is the rubric customizable?
faq.answer: The skill uses its included rubric by default. Users can provide extra review criteria for a specific project.
